title: Architectural drawings: a sheet, a set, and the building
intro: Architectural records are about a place — the building. This scenario branches on a single drawing vs a project drawing set, attaches the architect, and links the drawing to the building it depicts as a first-class Place, so the archive is searchable by site.
question: Is a drawing a Record and a drawing set a Record Set? How do I record the architect and the building?

steps.prompt: One drawing, or a whole project drawing set?

choices.label: A single drawing
choices.why: Good — one drawing (a plan, a section) is one Record; its vellum original and blueprint copies are Instantiations.

choices.label: A project drawing set
choices.why: Good — all drawings for a building project form a Record Set, each sheet a member Record.

choices.label: A Place
choices.why: Not the drawing. The building depicted is a Place you relate to the drawing — the drawing itself is a Record.

choices.label: Record
choices.why: Fits. The drawing is a Record. Its scale is an attribute; the original and blueprint copies are Instantiations; the building is a related Place.

choices.label: Record Set
choices.why: Too much for one sheet — a Set is for the project set.

choices.label: Instantiation
choices.why: That's the physical sheet/blueprint. The drawing's content is the Record.

capture.comment: Create the drawing as a Record.

body.title: Ground-floor plan — Civic Library, sheet A-02

choices.label: Record Set
choices.why: Fits. The drawing set aggregates all sheets for the project — a Record Set, each sheet a member Record.

choices.label: Record
choices.why: Undersells it — a set is many sheets (plans, sections, details). Use a Record Set.

choices.label: Record Part
choices.why: Doesn't fit — the set is an aggregation, not a component of one drawing.

capture.comment: Create the drawing set as a Record Set.

steps.prompt: Attach the architect (a person or a practice).

choices.label: Agent + has_creator
choices.why: Fits. The architect or practice is an Agent. Relate the drawing to them with has_creator; the same practice Agent links all its projects.

choices.label: Title block text only
choices.why: Doesn't fit for description. The title block is on the Instantiation; the architect should be a reconcilable Agent.

capture.comment: Create the architect / practice as an Agent.
Link the drawing to the architect with has_creator.

steps.prompt: The building the drawing depicts — how do you record it?

choices.label: Place + has_or_had_location
choices.why: Fits. The building/site is a Place (RiC-E22), related to the drawing with has_or_had_location. As a first-class Place it links every drawing, photo and record about that building, and can carry an address or coordinates.

choices.label: The building name in the title
choices.why: Doesn't fit. A building buried in a title can't be searched spatially or linked to other records about the same site. Model it as a Place.

choices.label: A Record
choices.why: Doesn't fit. The building is a location, not a record. Use a Place.

capture.comment: Create the building/site as a Place.
Link the drawing to the building with has_or_had_location.

outcome.verdict: Drawing, architect and building — searchable by site.
outcome.summary: The drawing is a Record (or the set a Record Set); the architect is a reusable Agent; and the building is a first-class Place linked by has_or_had_location. The archive becomes searchable by building — and every drawing, photo and report about that site links together.

links.label: See real RiC data in the graph viewer
